title: Solution Dilution Station
description: A chemistry lab workstation where the user must prepare a diluted solution by pouring purified water from a water bottle into a coffee mug repurposed as a beaker. The user controls the pour amount, adds a reagent tablet, stirs the mixture, and checks the concentration using a virtual pH color indicator that changes based on the dilution ratio.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive chemistry dilution station. Load coffeeMug.glb and WaterBottle.glb using GLTFLoader. Place the coffee mug at the center of a lab bench (a simple box geometry with a wood-tone material) and the water bottle to its right. Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 5) looking at the origin. Add a DirectionalLight from above-right and a soft AmbientLight for even illumination. Add a subtle grid or tiled floor beneath the bench. Interaction steps: (1) The user clicks the water bottle to pick it up — it animates upward and tilts 45 degrees toward the mug using TWEEN or requestAnimationFrame lerping. (2) A slider UI element appears labeled 'Pour Amount (mL)' ranging from 10 to 200. The user drags the slider, and a translucent blue cylinder inside the mug grows in height to represent the water level filling up. (3) The user clicks a 'Drop Reagent' button, which spawns a small colored sphere that falls into the mug with a simple gravity animation and dissolves (scales to zero). The water color shifts from blue toward green. (4) The user clicks a 'Stir' button, causing the water cylinder to rotate on its Y-axis with a swirl animation for 2 seconds. (5) A concentration readout panel appears showing the final dilution ratio and a color swatch that maps the pour amount to a pH-like gradient from red (concentrated) to green (ideal) to blue (over-diluted). Display a success message if the ratio falls within the ideal 80-120 mL range. Use OrbitControls for camera interaction. All UI elements should be HTML overlays styled with a clean lab-themed aesthetic using semi-transparent dark panels and monospace fonts.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  waterBottleTiltAngle: number,         // current tilt angle of water bottle in degrees (0-45)
  waterBottleLifted: boolean,         // whether water bottle has been picked up
  waterVolumeInMug: number,         // volume of water poured into mug in mL (0-200)
  reagentDropped: boolean,         // whether reagent tablet has been added to solution
  reagentDissolveProgress: number,         // dissolution progress of reagent (0.0-1.0)
  stirringActive: boolean,         // whether stirring animation is currently active
  stirRotationAngle: number,         // cumulative rotation angle during stirring in radians
  solutionHue: number,         // HSL hue value of solution color (180=blue, 120=green)
  concentrationPPM: number,         // calculated concentration in parts per million
  phIndicatorValue: number,         // pH indicator reading (1.0-14.0)
  stabilityTimer: number,         // milliseconds since last interaction for stability check
}
